schema_amendments.description: Array tracking derivation path for every constant or structural condition.
Boolean gate. Circularity detection: paths must terminate at live edges or IR axioms.

_meta.produced_by: Claude (Anthropic) — P4 Phase 4 Final Build Plan, S56
_meta.supersedes: P4-ai-layer v2 (S56 Phase 1) — Phase 3 corrections applied: F-4.2 restructured, IR renumbered, D-4.2 updated, P10-GAP-4.1 closed.

paper_title: Biological Systems: FRM Validation Across Biological Oscillatory Substrates

scope_boundary.IN: Non-neural biological oscillators with delayed negative feedback at cellular, tissue, organ, or organism level. Molecular oscillators in neural tissue (e.g., SCN circadian clock) are IN scope.
OUT: Neural circuits with synaptic feedback (P5 scope)
Limit-cycle oscillators with μ > 0 (FRM scope boundary)
scope_boundary.scope_note: P4/P5 boundary: if primary feedback mechanism is synaptic (neurotransmitter-mediated) → P5. If primary feedback mechanism is molecular (gene expression, protein concentration, ion channel) → P4.

inbound_edges.label: FRM functional form: f(t) = B + A·exp(−λt)·cos(ωt+φ)
T = 4·τ_gen circadian prediction
inbound_edges.received_as: Prior confirmation (P4 extends to all biological classes)

outbound_edges_register.label: Biological validation results for Act II integration consistency

biological_substrate_classes.description: Pre-specified validation set. Five classes (B1–B5). B3 is a scope boundary demonstration class. Section 5 alternative model mapping must be written before data analysis (C-3.ALT compliance). Phase 3 update: cross-class analysis requires ≥3 CONFIRMED systems per qualifying class.

classes.label: Circadian oscillators
classes.expected_result: Strong confirmation. P1 F-1.6 already predicts T = 24 hr.
classes.cross_class_role: Within-class replication. All B1 systems share TTFL mechanism. P1 confirmed 2 systems; P4 adds 3 new organisms (Drosophila, Neurospora, Arabidopsis) as replication across genomes. Cross-class independence comes from comparison with B2, B4, B5 (different feedback mechanisms).

classes.label: Cell cycle oscillators
systems: Xenopus laevis embryonic cell cycle (τ_gen = 30 min, P3 vacuity witness)

classes.label: Cardiac oscillators
classes.feedback_mechanism: Ion channel delayed negative feedback (voltage-gated K+ channels)
classes.tau_gen_sub_protocol: STRUCTURAL (refractory period / ion channel recovery time)
classes.expected_result: Partial. Sustained pacemaking is limit cycle (μ>0, OUT of scope). Post-perturbation recovery (μ<0) is IN scope. Most prominent systems expected EXCLUDED.
classes.scope_boundary_note: Pre-specify: sustained pacemaking → EXCLUDED (μ>0). Post-perturbation recovery → IN SCOPE (μ<0). C-3.DIAG handles classification.
classes.scope_boundary_class_note: Phase 3 Objection 3 resolution: B3 is a scope boundary demonstration class. B3 EXCLUDED systems demonstrate that C-3.DIAG correctly classifies limit cycles as OUT of scope. B3 does NOT count toward the cross-class minimum for F-4.2 or F-4.3 unless it yields ≥3 CONFIRMED systems. B3's primary contribution is scope boundary validation.

classes.label: Metabolic oscillators
classes.feedback_mechanism: Enzymatic delayed negative feedback (product inhibition, allosteric regulation)
classes.tau_gen_sub_protocol: STRUCTURAL (enzymatic reaction delay) or SPECTRAL (from oscillation period)
classes.expected_result: Partial. Some metabolic oscillators may be limit cycles (μ>0, OUT of scope).
classes.note: B4 has n=2 systems. Qualifies for individual fits (F-4.1) but excluded from cross-class analysis (F-4.2, F-4.3) unless additional systems are identified during Phase 4 literature survey.

classes.label: Musculoskeletal adaptation
classes.feedback_mechanism: Delayed negative feedback at tissue/organism level (stress → adaptation → recovery)
classes.tau_gen_sub_protocol: STRUCTURAL (recovery time from published exercise science literature)
classes.expected_result: Confirmation expected. Supercompensation data-contingent (PH-4.1). Wound healing and bone remodelling have published quantitative data.

inference_rules.name: Statistical Inference
inference_rules.form: Sample statistic within threshold → population property

inference_rules.name: Parsimony
inference_rules.form: Prefer simpler explanation when empirically equivalent

inference_rules.name: Protocol Specification
inference_rules.form: P3 measurement protocol step validity. Renamed from P3-local IR-12 (S56 Phase 3, Objection 2 resolution).

inference_rules.name: Statistical Standard Anchoring
inference_rules.form: Statistical threshold anchored at published standard. Renamed from P3-local IR-13 (S56 Phase 3, Objection 2 resolution).

claim_registry.label: FRM functional form
claim_registry.statement: f(t) = B + A·e^(−λt)·cos(ωt + φ) with ω = π/(2·τ_gen), λ ≈ |α|/(Γ·τ_gen), Γ = 1+π²/4, β = 1/2. Zero free parameters.

claim_registry.label: Universality class criteria
claim_registry.statement: The FRM universality class is defined by D-2.1: (a) delayed negative feedback with single dominant delay τ>0; (b) Hopf linearisation; (c) τ independently measurable.

claim_registry.label: Substrate independence of β
claim_registry.statement: β = 1/2 holds across all substrates satisfying D-2.1(a)–(c). Proved analytically by P2 C-2.1 (RG fixed-point) and confirmed independent of DDE form by P2 C-2.4.

claim_registry.label: Measurement protocol
claim_registry.statement: The P3 C-3.REG regression protocol (R1–R9) is the standard measurement method for FRM application papers. Zero-free-parameter constraint preserved. R²≥0.85 threshold. C-3.ALT alternative model comparison. C-3.DIAG scope diagnostics.

claim_registry.label: Biological substrate class definition
claim_registry.statement: A biological oscillatory system is eligible for P4 validation if: (a) it is a biological system (living organism or component thereof); (b) the primary oscillatory mechanism involves delayed negative feedback at the molecular, cellular, tissue, or organism level; (c) the primary feedback is NOT synaptic (neurotransmitter-mediated) — synaptic oscillators are P5 scope. Molecular oscillators in neural tissue (e.g., SCN circadian) are IN P4 scope.

claim_registry.label: Biological validation set
claim_registry.statement: Five biological substrate classes (B1–B5): B1 circadian oscillators, B2 cell cycle oscillators, B3 cardiac oscillators (scope boundary class), B4 metabolic oscillators, B5 musculoskeletal adaptation. Pre-specified before data analysis. Minimum 2 systems per class for individual fit inclusion (F-4.1). Minimum 3 CONFIRMED systems per class for cross-class analysis participation (F-4.2, F-4.3). Minimum 3 qualifying classes for cross-class claims. Scope boundary classes (B3) excluded from cross-class minimum unless ≥3 CONFIRMED systems.

claim_registry.label: Biological τ_gen instantiation
claim_registry.statement: τ_gen extraction for biological substrates follows P3 D-3.2 hierarchy. Class-specific instantiations: B1 = gene expression delay (TTFL period ÷ 4); B2 = CDK1/APC feedback delay; B3 = ion channel refractory period; B4 = enzymatic reaction delay or spectral extraction; B5 = recovery time from exercise science literature. τ_gen is never fitted to oscillation data.

claim_registry.label: Biological FRM goodness of fit
claim_registry.statement: The FRM functional form fits biological oscillators with mean R²_frm ≥ 0.85 across all tested biological substrate classes, using zero fitted parameters (P3 C-3.REG protocol).

falsification_predicate.FALSIFIED_IF: EXISTS biological substrate class BC IN {B1, B2, B3, B4, B5} SUCH THAT mean_R2_frm(BC) < 0.85 AND n_systems(BC) >= 2 AND all systems in BC satisfy D-2.1(a)(b)(c)

WHERE.mean_R2_frm: scalar · dimensionless · mean R²_frm across all D-3.1-eligible systems in class BC, computed per C-3.REG R4
WHERE.n_systems: integer · count of D-3.1-eligible systems tested in class BC

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: For each class BC: (1) verify D-3.1 eligibility for each system. (2) Apply C-3.REG R1–R9 with α=−1 default. (3) If R²_frm < 0.85 with α=−1: apply α-diagnostic (look up literature α, recompute). (4) Compute mean R²_frm across class using best available α per system. (5) Compare to 0.85 threshold. Finite, deterministic.
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: mean_R2_frm = 0.85 → NOT FALSIFIED (threshold inclusive). Classes with n_systems < 2 are EXCLUDED from class-level test (insufficient data, not falsifying). R²_frm threshold 0.85 from P3 C-3.REG R4 (Kvålseth 1985 anchor). α-diagnostic: if R² < 0.85 with α=−1 but R² ≥ 0.85 with literature α → CONFIRMED with α-sensitivity note (FRM form adequate, default was too crude). If R² < 0.85 with best available α → ANOMALOUS. Phase 3 Objection 7 resolution.
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: F-4.1 | Type F | application_C primary result | R² threshold from P3 | zero free parameters | class-level aggregation | minimum 2 systems per class | α-diagnostic separates form adequacy from parameter uncertainty
falsification_predicate.vacuity_witness: Cardiac pacemaker with sustained limit cycle (μ>0): D-2.1(b) fails (not near Hopf), R²_frm expected < 0.85. Correctly EXCLUDED by D-3.1. Predicate discriminates: system satisfying D-2.1 with genuinely poor fit would FALSIFY.

claim_registry.label: Spectral frequency consistency
claim_registry.statement: The observed dominant oscillation frequency ω_spectral matches the FRM-predicted frequency ω_predicted = π/(2·τ_gen) within 2σ_ω across all tested biological substrate classes with ≥3 CONFIRMED systems.

falsification_predicate.FALSIFIED_IF: EXISTS biological substrate class BC with n_confirmed(BC) ≥ 3 AND scope_boundary_class(BC) = false SUCH THAT |mean_ω_spectral(BC) − ω_predicted(BC)| > 2·σ_ω(BC)

WHERE.ω_spectral: scalar · rad/s · dominant frequency from Fourier analysis of O(t) for each system in BC
WHERE.ω_predicted: scalar · rad/s · π/(2·τ_gen) per system, where τ_gen extracted per D-4.3
WHERE.mean_ω_spectral: scalar · rad/s · mean observed ω_spectral across CONFIRMED systems in BC
WHERE.ω_predicted_mean: scalar · rad/s · mean ω_predicted = π/(2·τ_gen) across systems in BC
WHERE.σ_ω: scalar · rad/s · standard error of (ω_spectral − ω_predicted) within BC, computed by bootstrap (B=1000)
WHERE.n_confirmed: integer · count of CONFIRMED systems in BC per C-3.DIAG

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: For each qualifying class BC (n_confirmed ≥ 3, not scope_boundary_class): (1) extract ω_spectral from Fourier analysis of O(t) per system. (2) Compute ω_predicted = π/(2·τ_gen) per system from D-4.3. (3) Compute per-system deviation Δω = ω_spectral − ω_predicted. (4) Compute mean Δω and σ_ω via bootstrap. (5) Test |mean Δω| < 2·σ_ω. Finite.
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: 2σ_ω threshold: anchored at Bevington & Robinson 2003 §3.2 (95% CI). Classes with n_confirmed < 3 excluded from cross-class test (Phase 3 Objection 6 resolution). Scope boundary classes excluded unless ≥3 CONFIRMED.
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: F-4.2 | Type F | spectral consistency test | Phase 3 restructured from 'β substrate independence' (tautological, Objection 1) to spectral frequency consistency (genuine empirical content) | tests whether observed oscillation frequency matches FRM prediction from structural τ_gen | 2σ = 95% CI | extends P2 C-2.4 to empirical biological validation via observable frequency, not analytic β
falsification_predicate.vacuity_witness: Biological system with τ_gen = 6 hr (circadian): ω_predicted = π/43200 rad/s. If TTFL delay is misidentified as 3 hr: ω_predicted doubles, deviation from ω_spectral exceeds 2σ → FALSIFIED. System with non-FRM oscillatory mechanism (e.g., stochastic resonance): ω_spectral has no fixed relationship to τ_gen → systematic deviation → FALSIFIED. Discriminating. C6: PASS.

claim_registry.label: Biological T_char prediction
claim_registry.statement: T_char = 4·τ_gen matches the observed oscillation period T_obs in biological systems within 10% across all tested classes with ≥3 CONFIRMED systems.

falsification_predicate.FALSIFIED_IF: EXISTS biological substrate class BC with n_confirmed(BC) ≥ 3 AND scope_boundary_class(BC) = false SUCH THAT EXISTS system S in BC WHERE |T_char(S) − T_obs(S)| / T_obs(S) > 0.10

WHERE.T_char: scalar · seconds · 4·τ_gen, FRM prediction per C-3.REG R7
WHERE.T_obs: scalar · seconds · observed dominant period from time series of O(t)
WHERE.n_confirmed: integer · count of CONFIRMED systems in BC per C-3.DIAG

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: For each system S in qualifying classes (n_confirmed ≥ 3, not scope_boundary_class): (1) extract τ_gen per D-4.3. (2) Compute T_char = 4·τ_gen. (3) Measure T_obs from data. (4) Compute relative deviation. (5) Test against 10% threshold. Finite.
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: |T_char − T_obs| / T_obs = 0.10 → NOT FALSIFIED (threshold inclusive). 10% threshold anchored at 3σ detection threshold (Taylor 1997 §4.1) applied to combined measurement uncertainty σ_combined = √((δτ_gen/τ_gen)² + (δT_obs/T_obs)²) ≈ 2–4% for well-characterised biological oscillators. 10% ≈ 3×σ_combined (geometric mean). P10-GAP-4.1 CLOSED. Classes with n_confirmed < 3 or scope_boundary_class = true excluded from cross-class test.
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: F-4.3 | Type F | period prediction | zero-parameter prediction | extends P1 F-1.6 (circadian-only) to all biological classes | 10% threshold from Taylor (1997) 3σ detection | Phase 3 Objection 8 resolution closes P10-GAP-4.1
falsification_predicate.vacuity_witness: Circadian system with τ_gen = 6 hr: T_char = 24 hr, T_obs = 24 hr → deviation = 0%. System with incorrectly identified τ_gen (e.g., 3 hr): T_char = 12 hr, T_obs = 24 hr → deviation = 50% → FALSIFIED. Discriminating. C6: PASS.

claim_registry.label: Biological alternative model comparison
claim_registry.statement: The FRM performs within Δ ≥ −0.05 of the best alternative model for each biological substrate class, per C-3.ALT protocol.

falsification_predicate.FALSIFIED_IF: EXISTS biological substrate class BC SUCH THAT Δ(BC) < −0.05

WHERE.Δ: scalar · dimensionless · R²_frm − R²_alt, where R²_alt is from best alternative model pre-specified in P4 Section 3 mapping table
WHERE.best_alt_model: Pre-specified per class: B1 = Goodwin oscillator (Gonze et al. 2005); B2 = Novak-Tyson ODE; B3 = FitzHugh-Nagumo; B4 = Goldbeter allosteric; B5 = exponential recovery

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: For each class BC: (1) fit FRM per C-3.REG. (2) Fit pre-specified alternative. (3) Compute Δ = R²_frm − R²_alt. (4) Test Δ ≥ −0.05. Finite.
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: Δ = −0.05 → NOT FALSIFIED (threshold inclusive). Δ < −0.05 means alternative substantially outperforms FRM. Threshold from P3 C-3.ALT (Burnham & Anderson 2002).
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: F-4.4 | Type F | model comparison | alternative models pre-specified (C-3.ALT compliance) | Δ ≥ −0.05 per class | alternative models have fitted parameters (k=5–15); FRM has zero (k=0) | comparison is asymmetric by design: tests whether zero-parameter FRM competes with fitted alternatives — a stronger result than R² alone suggests (Phase 3 Objection 9 resolution) | AIC comparison would favour FRM even more strongly: AIC_FRM = n·ln(SS_res/n) (k=0) vs AIC_alt = n·ln(SS_res_alt/n) + 2k_alt — for similar R² and k_alt≥5, ΔAIC strongly favours FRM | R² comparison is conservative toward FRM
falsification_predicate.vacuity_witness: Detailed mechanistic model with 10+ fitted parameters could achieve R²_alt > R²_frm + 0.05 by overfitting. If this occurs for a D-2.1-satisfying system: genuinely informative — suggests FRM misses substrate-specific dynamics. C6: PASS.

claim_registry.label: Supercompensation as FRM instance
claim_registry.statement: The Rippetoe/Selye stress-adaptation supercompensation curve follows f(t) = B + A·e^(−λt)·cos(ωt + φ) with τ_gen = published recovery time, R²≥0.85, and T_char = 4·τ_gen matching the observed supercompensation period.

WHERE.R2_frm_supercomp: scalar · dimensionless · R²_frm for supercompensation time series per C-3.REG
WHERE.T_char_supercomp: scalar · hours · 4·τ_gen where τ_gen = published recovery time (e.g., 48–72 hr for novice resistance training)
WHERE.T_obs_supercomp: scalar · hours · observed time to peak supercompensation from quantitative exercise science data

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: (1) Identify τ_gen from published exercise science (recovery time). (2) Compute FRM prediction. (3) Fit to quantitative supercompensation data. (4) Compute R²_frm. (5) Compare T_char to T_obs. Finite.
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: R²_frm = 0.85 → NOT FALSIFIED. T_char deviation = 10% → NOT FALSIFIED. Both thresholds inclusive.
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: F-4.5 | Type F | specific biological application | Rippetoe model as FRM instance | Build Table explicitly cites stress-adaptation as biological-scale FRM evidence | quantitative data required — qualitative descriptions insufficient | DATA-CONTINGENT: PH-4.1 flags data availability (Phase 3 Objection 5 resolution)
falsification_predicate.vacuity_witness: Pure exponential recovery (no oscillatory component): R²_frm < 0.85 for FRM but R²_alt > 0.95 for exponential. If supercompensation is pure exponential: FALSIFIED. Discriminating. C6: PASS.

placeholder_register.description: Supercompensation quantitative data. Requires published time-series data of strength/performance recovery after training stimulus with sufficient temporal resolution (≥6 data points during recovery window) to resolve oscillatory dynamics. Qualitative Rippetoe/Selye models exist but quantitative time-series may be limited.
candidate_data_sources: Zatsiorsky & Kraemer (2006) — quantitative strength recovery data
placeholder_register.phase_3_note: Objection 5 resolution: if no quantitative time-series data exists with sufficient temporal resolution, F-4.5 → PH-4.1 BLOCKED and B5 relies on wound healing and bone remodelling only.

placeholder_register.description: Full biological dataset assembly. Per-class fits not yet computed. Requires literature data for all five substrate classes. Phase 4 task.

principle_10_audit.derivation_path: Inherited from P3 C-3.REG R4. Anchored at Kvålseth (1985, Am. Statistician 39:279). P10-GAP-3.1 CLOSED S48.

Inherited from P1 F-1.3 and P2 C-2.4 for cross-paper comparability. Anchored at Bevington & Robinson 2003 §3.2. P10-GAP-2.5 CLOSED S48 by P3 C-3.σ. Phase 3: applied to ω_spectral vs ω_predicted (restructured F-4.2).

Anchored at Taylor (1997, Introduction to Error Analysis §4.1) 3σ detection threshold. Combined measurement uncertainty σ_combined = √((δτ_gen/τ_gen)² + (δT_obs/T_obs)²) ≈ 2–4% for well-characterised biological oscillators. 10% ≈ 3×σ_combined (geometric mean of range 6–15%). IR-14 (Statistical Standard Anchoring) satisfied. Phase 3 Objection 8 resolution.
Inherited from P3 C-3.ALT. Anchored at Burnham & Anderson (2002, Model Selection §2.5). P10-GAP-3.2 CLOSED S48.

principle_10_audit.constant_or_condition: n ≥ 3 CONFIRMED systems per class for cross-class analysis
principle_10_audit.derivation_path: Minimum sample size for meaningful standard error computation. At n=2, 95% CI spans ±12.7σ (t-distribution, df=1) — test has no power. At n=3, CI spans ±4.3σ (df=2) — still limited but computable. Phase 3 Objection 6 resolution. IR-14 satisfied via t-distribution properties.

principle_10_compliant_note: All P10 gaps closed. P10-GAP-4.1 closed S56 Phase 3 (Taylor 1997 3σ detection threshold). n≥3 minimum anchored at t-distribution properties.

phase_ready.note: Phase 4 Final Build Plan complete S56. 12 claims (4A + 3D + 5F). All Phase 3 corrections applied. F-4.2 restructured (spectral consistency). P10-GAP-4.1 closed. IR numbering resolved. principle_10_compliant: true. 2 placeholders (PH-4.1 POTENTIALLY_BLOCKING, PH-4.2 blocking). Phase 5 (CBT I-9) pending.

github_publication.status: Phase 4 — NOT YET PUBLISHABLE (Phase 5 CBT I-9 pending)

key_contrast_sources.contrast: ODE models with fitted parameters. FRM: zero free parameters.
Detailed mechanistic ODE models. FRM: universality class, not mechanism.
Multi-variable Goodwin oscillator. FRM: single functional form.
Qualitative stress-adaptation model. FRM: quantitative, predictive.
DDE theory in biology. No universality prediction (β=1/2).
GAS model (qualitative). FRM: mathematical form with derived constants.
Topological analysis. FRM: metric, not topological.
Error analysis standard. Anchors P10-GAP-4.1 closure (3σ detection threshold).

prior_art.novelty_claim: No prior work derives biological oscillation dynamics from a universality-class argument with zero free parameters and substrate-independent critical exponents.

corrections_applied: F-4.2: restructured from 'β substrate independence' (tautological) to 'spectral frequency consistency' (genuine empirical content)
IR-12→IR-13, IR-13→IR-14: canonical renumbering to resolve collision with schema IR-12 (Causal Precedence)
D-4.2: cross-class minimum raised from n≥2 to n≥3 CONFIRMED systems per class
B3: flagged as scope_boundary_class (excluded from cross-class minimum)
F-4.1: α-diagnostic sub-clause added to EVALUATION and BOUNDARY
F-4.4: AIC note and asymmetry framing added to CONTEXT
PH-4.1: severity upgraded to POTENTIALLY_BLOCKING with candidate data sources
P2 inbound edges: marked PROVISIONAL (P2 Phase 1 only)
